title: canthium scabridum
intro: Canthium scabridum, also known as the Rough-stemmed Canthium, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, characterized by its woody stems and small, white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, derived from its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of Canthium scabridum are said to include relief from fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as aid in wound healing and the management of various skin conditions. In traditional herbal medicine, Canthium scabridum is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it can also be used as a topical treatment in the form of a paste or ointment.
